pattern: Three independent fixed-capacity counters, one per car size, are all a parking system needs: each admission just checks and decrements the one counter that matches the car's size.
hints: There's no need for any data structure beyond three numbers, one slot-count per car size.
Admitting a car only ever touches the counter matching that car's own size; the other two counters are irrelevant to that decision.
On addCar, check whether the matching size still has room: if so, decrement it and return true; otherwise return false without changing anything.

edge_inputs.description: fills the single small slot then rejects the next small car
every capacity is zero, every car is rejected
a single slot of each size, one admitted then the same size rejected
rejecting one car type must not affect the other two counters
large capacity for one size handles many repeated admissions

stress.description: long randomized sequence of addCar calls against small random capacities
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    big = rng.randint(0, 3)
    medium = rng.randint(0, 3)
    small = rng.randint(0, 3)
    ops = ['ParkingSystem']
    args = [[big, medium, small]]
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    for _ in range(n):
        ops.append('addCar')
        args.append([rng.randint(1, 3)])
    return [ops, args]
